Life Sciences & Molecular Discovery:
## Category Timeline: Life Sciences & Molecular Discovery

### SUB-THEME A: Protein Engineering & Structural Biology (2021→2025, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Atomic-level Structure Prediction"** (2021–2024): Established by **AlphaFold**, providing the first computational method for predicting 3D protein structures with atomic accuracy, shifting the field from experimental-only to computation-first.
- **"Biomolecular Interaction Modeling"** (2024–2025): Transition from static structures to dynamic interactions with **Boltz-1**, an open-source model democratizing the modeling of complex molecular interactions.
- **"De Novo Antibody Design"** (2024–2025): Utilizing **RFdiffusion** to fine-tune generative models for the creation of VHH and scFv antibodies targeting specific epitopes with atomic precision.
- **"Bioinformatics Foundation Models"** (2025–2025): The integration of large-scale **Foundation Models (FMs)** to unify disparate biological data types into shared latent spaces for downstream discovery.
Interaction: **FEEDS** structural data into Drug Discovery pipelines and **ENABLES** the high-fidelity simulations used by Virtual Lab agents.

### SUB-THEME B: Autonomous Drug Discovery & Asset Management (2022→2026, ACCELERATING)
Branches into:
- **"DMTA Cycle Automation"** (2022–2025): Implementation of the **Tippy** framework, utilizing a Multi-Agent approach (Supervisor, Manager, Worker) to automate the Design-Make-Test-Analyze cycle in the pharmaceutical industry.
- **"Cross-drug Response Evaluation"** (2023–2025): Development of **DeepCRE** to transform R&D by evaluating multi-drug responses through AI-driven predictive modeling.
- **"Global Asset Scouting"** (2025–2026): The emergence of the **Bioptic Agent**, a tree-based self-learning AI system designed for "Hunt Globally" missions to scout and evaluate drug assets for investment.
- **"Closed-Loop Multi-Objective Optimization"** (2025–2026): Introduction of **MAC-AMP**, a closed-loop multi-agent collaboration system designed to balance conflicting objectives in molecular optimization.
Interaction: **RESPONDS** to structural breakthroughs from Sub-theme A and **MERGES** with Agentic Genomics to validate therapeutic targets.

### SUB-THEME C: Agentic Genomics & Systems Biology (2023→2026, EMERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Automated Systems Biology"** (2023–2025): Early frameworks like **Genesis** moving toward the full automation of systems biology research through iterative hypothesis generation.
- **"Interactive Aging Research"** (2024–2026): The **PersonaAI** framework, utilizing RAG and 560,000+ documents to create an interactive agentic environment for autonomous aging hypotheses.
- **"Single-Cell LLM Scaling"** (2025–2026): Scaling Large Language Models for next-generation analysis via **Cell2Sentence (C2S)**, representing scRNA-seq profiles as sequences for generative modeling.
- **"Autonomous ML for Omics"** (2025–2026): **Agentomics-ML**, an autonomous machine learning agent that automates experimentation for genomic and transcriptomic classification.
- **"Gene Ontology Discovery"** (2026–2026): Utilizing the **Virtual Study Group** and hierarchical feature selection to revisit and discover new knowledge within Gene Ontology (GO) terms.
Interaction: **SPAWNS** specialized agents for behavioral neuroscience and **ENABLES** the "In Silico" science of the mind.

### SUB-THEME D: Virtual Labs & Cognitive Automation (2024→2026, EXPANDING)
Branches into:
- **"Multimodal Pathology Copilots"** (2024–2025): Development of generative AI assistants for human pathology to integrate visual and textual clinical data.
- **"Multi-Agent Nanobody Design"** (2024–2025): The **Virtual Lab** (Stanford/James Zou), where teams of AI agents (PI, Immunologist, Computational Biologist) design new SARS-CoV-2 nanobodies.
- **"Behavioral Neuroscience Discovery"** (2025–2026): Transforming discovery through **In-Context Learning**, allowing agents to interpret complex behavioral data without extensive retraining.
- **"In Silico Cognitive Science"** (2026–2026): The full automation of the cognitive science discovery cycle—from experimental design to data collection—aiming for a complete "science of the mind" within AI.
Interaction: **ENABLES** end-to-end scientific discovery by integrating tools from all other sub-themes into a unified agentic workflow.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- **Agentic Orchestration**: The shift from single-model tools (AlphaFold) to multi-agent systems (Tippy, Virtual Lab) is the primary driver of acceleration across all themes.
- **Data Democratization**: Open-source models like **Boltz-1** and **Agentomics-ML** are narrowing the gap between high-resource pharmaceutical labs and academic research.
- **Clinical Validation**: The transition from "in silico" design to "clinical review" is bridged by LLM platforms that generate human-comparable clinical reviews.

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2021: AlphaFold Release** — The milestone of atomic-level protein structure prediction that catalyzed the computational biology revolution.
- **2024: The Virtual Lab Launch** — The first successful demonstration of a multi-agent AI team designing viable SARS-CoV-2 nanobodies, marking the birth of the "AI Scientist."
- **2026: Global Asset Scouting** — The deployment of **Bioptic Agent**, shifting AI's role from molecular design to the strategic management of global pharmaceutical assets.

▶ **Agentic Science** emerges (**2024**)
◀ **Manual DMTA Cycles** absorbed into **Autonomous Frameworks** (**2025**)
Physical Sciences & Engineering:
## Category Timeline: Physical Sciences & Engineering

### SUB-THEME A: Physics-Informed Machine Learning & Operators (2017→2026, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Foundational PINNs"** (2017–2022): Establishment of Physics-Informed Neural Networks for solving supervised learning tasks while respecting the law of physics (nonlinear PDEs).
- **"Neural Operator Frameworks"** (2020–2025): Development of DeepONet, FNO, and PCANet to approximate solution operators for parametric PDEs, moving beyond point-wise approximations.
- **"Uncertainty & Simulation Inference"** (2020–2023): Integration of simulation-based inference (SBI) and comprehensive uncertainty quantification (UQ) frameworks for scientific ML.
- **"Infinite-Dimensional & Spiking Operators"** (2025–2026): Emergence of Neural-POD for mesh-independent nonlinear bases and SPINONet for energy-efficient spiking physics-informed operators.
- **"Continual & Task-Agnostic Learning"** (2024–2026): Implementation of SLE-FNO (Single-Layer Extensions) to allow neural operators to adapt to new physical tasks without forgetting.
Interaction: **FEEDS** Sub-theme C by providing the surrogate models necessary for real-time autonomous control and optimization.

### SUB-THEME B: AI-Accelerated Materials Discovery (2019→2026, ACCELERATING)
Branches into:
- **"Large-Scale Structural Discovery"** (2023–2024): Massive expansion of stable crystal structures using GNoME and active learning, scaling from thousands to hundreds of thousands of candidates.
- **"Domain-Specific Foundation Models"** (2024–2025): Development of MatterChat (multi-modal LLM) and specialized fine-tuning (CPT, SFT, ORPO) for material science adaptation.
- **"Generative Matter Modeling"** (2025–2026): Introduction of DMFlow for disordered materials generation and MDtrajNet for direct prediction of molecular dynamics trajectories.
- **"Automated Knowledge Extraction"** (2025–2026): Use of LLM chains like L2M3 to mine synthesis conditions and properties from tens of thousands of legacy papers (e.g., MOFs).
- **"Polymer & Semiconductor Workflows"** (2024–2026): Integration of AI/ML into the synthesis-simulation-characterization pipeline for polymer brushes and organic semiconductor devices.
Interaction: **MERGES** with Sub-theme C as generative designs are directly fed into robotic synthesis platforms.

### SUB-THEME C: Autonomous Scientific Agents & Labs (2023→2026, EMERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Multi-Agent Simulation Orchestration"** (2024–2025): Frameworks like VASPilot, OpenFOAMGPT 2.0, and MechAgents that use LLM agents to automate end-to-end CFD, DFT, and solid mechanics workflows.
- **"Autonomous Chemical Synthesis"** (2023–2025): Development of LLM-RDF and low-cost robotic platforms for mapping chemical reaction hyperspaces and automating synthesis development.
- **"AI-Native Physical Facilities"** (2025–2026): Transitioning large-scale hardware, such as particle accelerators, into fully autonomous, self-driving facilities.
- **"Automated Code & Solver Engineering"** (2025–2026): AutoNumerics and LLM-ODE systems that autonomously design, debug, and implement numerical solvers for complex dynamical systems.
- **"Vision-Language Characterization"** (2026): Use of EAA (Automating Materials Characterization) to interpret experimental data through multi-modal vision-language models.
Interaction: **RESPONDS** to the data explosion from Sub-theme B by providing the throughput necessary to validate AI-generated hypotheses.

### SUB-THEME D: Computational Physics & Exascale Dynamics (2020→2025, EVOLVING)
Branches into:
- **"Exascale Simulation Paradigms"** (2023–2025): Adapting complex physical simulations to utilize the next generation of exascale computing architectures.
- **"Quantum & High-Energy Phenomena"** (2024–2025): Investigating chiral spin symmetry in hot QCD and nonlinear stochastic motion derived from Coulomb forces.
- **"Astrophysical Signal Inference"** (2024–2025): Utilizing post-merger signal analysis to distinguish between neutron stars and low-mass black hole binaries.
- **"Molecular Dynamics Toolkits"** (2024–2025): Development of open-source toolkits like AMDAT for specialized analysis of supercooled liquids and molecular systems.
Interaction: **SPAWNS** the high-fidelity datasets required to train the foundation models in Sub-theme A and B.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- **Surrogate-Driven Discovery**: Neural operators (Sub-theme A) replace expensive solvers in materials discovery loops (Sub-theme B), enabling million-fold speedups in screening.
- **Agentic Simulation**: LLM agents (Sub-theme C) now act as the interface for complex computational physics codes (Sub-theme D), lowering the barrier for multi-physics optimization.
- **Data-Centric Feedback**: Autonomous labs (Sub-theme C) generate structured experimental data that is fed back into domain-specific LLMs (Sub-theme B) to refine predictive accuracy.

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2017: The PINN Breakthrough** — Introduction of Physics-Informed Neural Networks, bridging deep learning with differential equations.
- **2023: Scaling Materials Discovery** — GNoME demonstrates that deep learning can discover stable materials at a scale orders of magnitude beyond human capability.
- **2026: The AI-Native Facility** — Transition from "AI-assisted" to "AI-native" infrastructure, where particle accelerators and labs operate autonomously from design to execution.

▶ Physics-Informed Machine Learning emerges (2017)
◀ Manual Simulation Setup absorbed into Multi-Agent Automation (2025)
Autonomous Agents & Research Workflow:
## Category Timeline: Autonomous Agents & Research Workflow

### SUB-THEME A: End-to-End Research Automation & Discovery Loops (2023→2026, ACCELERATING)
Branches into:
- **"Hypothesis Generation & Ideation"** (2023–2024): Early systems focusing on literature-based discovery and link prediction, such as **SciMON** and **PaperRobot**, which transitioned from simple knowledge graph mining to LLM-driven novelty optimization.
- **"Autonomous Research Pipelines"** (2024–2025): The emergence of fully integrated systems like **The AI Scientist** and **Agent Laboratory** that automate the entire lifecycle from ideation to code execution and paper drafting.
- **"Iterative Refinement & Closed-Loop Discovery"** (2024–2026): Development of frameworks like **CycleResearcher** and **InternAgent** that utilize feedback loops to refine research outputs, moving toward "Agentic Science" where the AI manages the full experimental cycle.
- **"Advanced Innovation Engines"** (2025–2026): High-tier systems such as **AI-Researcher** and **The AI Scientist-v2** capable of workshop-level discovery and autonomous algorithmic innovation with minimal human intervention.
- **"Embodied Science & Physical Loops"** (2026): The final frontier where digital agents integrate with physical hardware, as seen in **Embodied Science**, to close the loop between computational hypothesis and robotic experimentation.
Interaction: **ENABLES** Sub-theme D by providing the raw research outputs that require formal verification and **FEEDS** Sub-theme C with specific task requirements for tool execution.

### SUB-THEME B: Multi-Agent Orchestration & Collaborative Intelligence (2023→2026, ACCELERATING)
Branches into:
- **"Task Decomposition & Planning"** (2023): Initial frameworks like **HuggingGPT** that used LLMs as central planners to orchestrate specialized expert models for complex scientific tasks.
- **"Multi-Disciplinary Collaboration"** (2023–2024): Systems like **MedAgents** and **SciAgents** that simulate "expert panels" or bio-inspired agent swarms to solve cross-domain problems through dialogue and consensus.
- **"Virtual Laboratories & Shared Workspaces"** (2024–2025): The rise of environments like the **Virtual Lab** and **AgentRxiv**, where multiple agents collaborate, search, and reference each other's work via shared preprint-style servers.
- **"Agentic Scaling & Workforce Learning"** (2024–2026): Research into the quantitative principles of agent systems, such as **Towards a Science of Scaling Agent Systems** and **OWL (Optimized Workforce Learning)**, focusing on how agent populations improve performance.
- **"Human-Agent Collaborative Workflows"** (2025–2026): Methodological shifts in social sciences and humanities, exemplified by the **Anthropic Economic Index (AEI)** experiments, moving from AI-as-labor to AI-as-collaborator.
Interaction: **SPAWNS** the complex organizational structures used in Sub-theme A and **RESPONDS** to the scaling needs identified in Sub-theme C.

### SUB-THEME C: Tool-Use, API Integration & Domain Execution (2023→2025, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Chemical & Material Science Agents"** (2023–2024): Early domain-specific successes like **ChemCrow** and **Coscientist**, which integrated LLMs with robotic lab equipment and chemical databases.
- **"Universal Tool & API Frameworks"** (2023–2025): Efforts to scale agent capabilities to thousands of real-world tools, led by **ToolLLM**, **AnyTool**, and the **ToolUniverse** ecosystem.
- **"Code-Centric Research Agents"** (2025): Specialized agents for the "Paper-to-Code" transition, such as **ResearchCodeAgent** and **AutoP2C**, focusing on the technical implementation of scientific ideas.
- **"Specialized Scientific Verticals"** (2025): The diversification of agents into niche fields, including **Earth-Agent** for observation data, **Lang-PINN** for physics-informed neural networks, and **TxAgent** for therapeutic reasoning.
- **"Autonomous Data Science"** (2025): The convergence of data analysis and agentic reasoning in systems like **DeepAnalyze-8B**, designed for end-to-end autonomous data exploration.
Interaction: **MERGES** with Sub-theme A to provide the functional "hands" for autonomous labs and **ENABLES** Sub-theme B by expanding the library of skills available to agent swarms.

### SUB-THEME D: Formal Verification, Math & Scientific Rigor (2024→2026, EMERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Automated Scholarly Review (ASPR)"** (2024–2025): Systems like **ReviewerGPT**, **MARG**, and **Reviewer2** that automate the peer-review process, focusing on bias reduction and coverage.
- **"Mathematical Discovery & Reasoning"** (2025–2026): Transition from competition math to professional research math, highlighted by DeepMind’s **Aletheia** agent.
- **"Auto-formalization & Proofs"** (2026): Frameworks like **MerLean** and **M2F (Math-to-Formal)** that automatically convert LaTeX research papers into Lean 4 formal verification code.
- **"SciOps & Reproducibility"** (2026): The implementation of DevOps principles in science, such as **DataJoint 2.0** and **Scaling Reproducibility** workflows, ensuring that agent-generated research is verifiable and reproducible.
Interaction: **RESPONDS** to the high volume of output from Sub-theme A by acting as a quality gate and **ENABLES** the "Embodied Science" phase of Sub-theme A through rigorous safety and verification protocols.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- **The Feedback Loop**: Sub-theme D (Review) provides the critical reinforcement signal for Sub-theme A (Automation) to improve its generative quality over time.
- **The Tool-Agent Interface**: Sub-theme C (Tools) provides the necessary infrastructure for Sub-theme B (Orchestration) to move beyond text-based dialogue into real-world action.
- **The Scaling Paradox**: Insights from Sub-theme B regarding agent scaling laws directly influence the architectural design of the end-to-end systems in Sub-theme A.

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2024: The Birth of the AI Scientist** — The release of **The AI Scientist** marks the first successful demonstration of a fully automated, open-ended scientific discovery loop.
- **2025: The Agentic Shift** — Multiple comprehensive surveys (e.g., **"From AI for Science to Agentic Science"**) codify the transition from AI as a computational tool to AI as an autonomous scientific agent.
- **2026: Formalization at Scale** — The launch of **M2F** and **Aletheia** signals the move toward a future where scientific literature is automatically verified and formalized upon publication.

▶ **Agentic Science** emerges (**2024**)
◀ **Computational AI for Science** absorbed into **Autonomous Agentic Workflows** (**2025**)
Benchmarks & Evaluation:
## Category Timeline: Benchmarks & Evaluation

### SUB-THEME A: Scientific Reasoning & Discovery Benchmarks (2023→2026, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Foundational Scientific Reasoning"** (2023–2024): Focuses on systematic examination of college-level problem-solving and reasoning capabilities using datasets like **SciBench**.
- **"Research-Grade Coding & Data Readiness"** (2024–2025): Transitions from general reasoning to domain-specific implementation, utilizing **SciCode** for scientist-curated coding problems and **SciHorizon** for assessing AI-for-Science data readiness.
- **"Trustworthiness & Rigorous Assessment"** (2024–2025): Establishes frameworks for evaluating the reliability of LLMs in scientific contexts through **SciTrust** and **ScienceAgentBench**, focusing on data-driven discovery automation.
- **"Scenario-Grounded Multi-Disciplinary Discovery"** (2025–2026): Culminates in comprehensive evaluations across biology, chemistry, and materials science as seen in **Evaluating Large Language Models in Scientific Discovery**.
Interaction: This sub-theme FEEDS into Agentic Environments by providing the ground-truth logic required for autonomous experimentation.

### SUB-THEME B: Autonomous Agent & Tool-Use Environments (2023→2026, ACCELERATING)
Branches into:
- **"Software Engineering & Issue Resolution"** (2023–2024): Initial focus on real-world problem solving in GitHub repositories using **SWE-bench**.
- **"Scientific Tool-Use Gymnasiums"** (2024–2025): Development of interactive environments like **Aviary** and **SciAgentGym** to benchmark multi-step tool manipulation in research settings.
- **"Stable Tool Learning & Scalability"** (2025–2025): Addressing API instability and evaluation randomness through **StableToolBench** and scaling to domain-specific coding in **MedAgentGym**.
- **"Long-Term Coherence & Autonomous Operations"** (2025–2026): Testing agent persistence in simulated environments like **Vending-Bench** and **AIRS-Bench**.
- **"End-to-End Research Autonomy"** (2026–2026): The emergence of **ResearchGym**, enabling full-cycle hypothesis generation, experimental design, and evaluation.
Interaction: This sub-theme SPAWNS from Software Engineering benchmarks and ENABLES the transition from passive evaluation to active research agents.

### SUB-THEME C: Human-AI Comparative Performance (2023→2025, STABILIZING)
Branches into:
- **"Annotation & Classification Efficiency"** (2023–2024): Comparing LLMs against crowd workers for text-annotation tasks, showing **ChatGPT** outperforming traditional human-in-the-loop methods.
- **"Complex Textual & Linguistic Analysis"** (2024–2024): Evaluating LLMs against outsourced human coders for specialized tasks like NLP-based news analysis.
- **"Creative & Divergent Thinking"** (2024–2025): Assessing the limits of AI in creative tasks like the **Alternative Uses Task**, where top-tier humans still maintain an edge over chatbots.
- **"Novelty in Research Idea Generation"** (2025–2025): Large-scale human studies comparing LLM-generated research ideas against expert NLP researchers to determine "blind" quality scores.
Interaction: This sub-theme RESPONDS to the benchmarks in Sub-theme A by providing a human-centric baseline for "novelty" and "creativity."

### SUB-THEME D: Specialized Automation & Utility Benchmarking (2024→2026, DIVERSIFYING)
Branches into:
- **"AutoML Framework Assessment"** (2024–2024): Practical evaluation of 16 **AutoML tools** (AutoGluon, TPOT, etc.) across diverse classification tasks.
- **"Academic Workflow Automation"** (2024–2025): Benchmarking AI as peer reviewers for survey paper relevance via **RelevAI-Reviewer**.
- **"Content Transformation & Accessibility"** (2025–2026): Evaluating the conversion of static research into interactive formats through **Paper2Web**.
Interaction: This sub-theme MERGES with Agentic Environments to provide specialized "skills" for the end-to-end research pipeline.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- **Agentic Integration**: The shift from static benchmarks (**SciBench**) to interactive environments (**ResearchGym**) represents a fundamental move toward evaluating "doing" rather than just "knowing."
- **Human-Baseline Calibration**: Comparative studies (Human vs. AI) are increasingly used to validate the "rigor" of new scientific benchmarks like **ScienceAgentBench**.
- **Stability in Evaluation**: The move from **ToolBench** to **StableToolBench** highlights a field-wide push for reproducible and reliable agent evaluation metrics.

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2023: The Software Engineering Pivot** — **SWE-bench** establishes that LLMs can be evaluated on real-world, multi-file GitHub issues, moving beyond isolated code snippets.
- **2024: The Scientific Tool-Use Explosion** — The release of **Aviary** and **SciAgentGym** marks the transition of LLM agents from general assistants to specialized scientific instruments.
- **2026: Full-Cycle Research Autonomy** — **ResearchGym** emerges as the first comprehensive environment for evaluating the entire scientific method (Hypothesis → Evaluation) within a single framework.

▶ Scientific Agentic Research emerges (2024)
◀ Human-only annotation absorbed into LLM-driven pipelines (2024)

### ABSOLUTE VISUAL RULES
- Every ribbon MUST have its sub-theme name labeled
- English only, horizontal left→right, years on top
- River delta style, organic flowing ribbons
- White background, sans-serif, 16:9
- NO title, NO watercolor, NO paper counts, NO paper titles
- Show band WIDTH changes
Scientific NLP & Document Intelligence:
## Category Timeline: Scientific NLP & Document Intelligence

### SUB-THEME A: Scientific Knowledge Extraction & Structured Mining (2019→2026, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Foundational NLP Infrastructure"** (2019–2021): Development of robust, domain-specific pipelines like **scispaCy** and **spaCy** to handle specialized biomedical and technical vocabularies.
- **"Extreme Summarization & Metadata"** (2020–2023): Transition from full-text indexing to high-compression formats like **TLDR** generation and the scaling of discovery platforms like **Semantic Scholar**.
- **"Scholarly Knowledge Graphs & QA"** (2023–2025): Integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) with structured data, exemplified by **Scholarly KGQA** to answer complex bibliographic queries.
- **"Agentic Domain-Specific Extraction"** (2025–2026): Emergence of autonomous frameworks for deep technical mining, such as the **Automated Extraction of Mechanical Constitutive Models** and **Agentic Personas** for adaptive explanations.
Interaction: **FEEDS** high-quality structured data into Sub-theme C to facilitate automated literature reviews and **ENABLES** the grounding of multimodal models in Sub-theme B.

### SUB-THEME B: Multimodal Document Intelligence & Chart Reasoning (2021→2026, ACCELERATING)
Branches into:
- **"Large-scale Figure Captioning"** (2021–2023): Initial dataset creation for scientific imagery, including **SciCap**, **PatFig** (patent drawings), and knowledge-augmented versions like **SciCap+**.
- **"Visual Instruction Tuning"** (2023–2024): Rapid expansion of multimodal LLMs specialized for charts, featuring **ChartInstruct**, **ChartGemma**, **ChartLlama**, and the **MMC** dataset.
- **"Evidence-based Fact Checking"** (2024–2025): Utilizing visual data as primary evidence for verification, led by the **Chart-AFC** framework and hallucination correction via **Pelican**.
- **"Automated Scientific Illustration"** (2025–2026): Shift from understanding to generation with **PaperBanana** for automated diagramming and **Chartist** for task-driven visual control.
Interaction: **SPAWNS** new verification requirements for Sub-theme C by providing visual evidence that must be reconciled with textual claims.

### SUB-THEME C: Automated Synthesis & Research Lifecycle Support (2022→2026, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Human-AI Collaborative Writing"** (2022–2024): Early exploration of interactive drafting using the **CoAuthor** dataset and preference-optimized captioning in **FigCaps-HF**.
- **"Systematic Review & Synthesis Agents"** (2023–2025): Deployment of **Language Agents** for superhuman synthesis and specialized tools for **Systematic Review (El)**.
- **"Bias & Hallucination Mitigation"** (2024–2025): Critical analysis of **Generalization Bias** in scientific summaries and the development of **LimGen** for generating research limitations.
- **"Research Workflow Optimization"** (2024–2026): Practical integration of AI into the lab, as seen in **Nature's Guide to AI Tools** and AI-powered science search engines.
Interaction: **RESPONDS** to the extraction capabilities of Sub-theme A and the visual insights of Sub-theme B to produce comprehensive, multi-source scientific narratives.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- **Multimodal Grounding**: The convergence of text-based extraction (Sub-theme A) and chart reasoning (Sub-theme B) allows for "Criteria-first" structure discovery in scientific images.
- **Agentic Autonomy**: Agent-based frameworks are unifying the pipeline from raw document mining to the final synthesis of "AI Scientists."

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2019: The scispaCy Release** — Established the first industrial-strength NLP infrastructure specifically tuned for scientific literature.
- **2023: The Multimodal Shift** — Transition from text-only analysis to unified chart/figure understanding with the launch of **ChartReader** and **ChartLlama**.
- **2026: Agentic Synthesis** — The emergence of **PaperBanana** and **Agentic Personas** marks the shift from AI as a tool to AI as an autonomous research collaborator.
▶ Scientific NLP emerges (2019)
◀ Manual Systematic Review absorbed into Agentic Synthesis (2025)

### ABSOLUTE VISUAL RULES
- The timeline is represented as a horizontal flow from left (2019) to right (2026).
- Each sub-theme (Scientific Knowledge Extraction, Multimodal Document Intelligence, Automated Synthesis) is a distinct, organic ribbon.
- Ribbon widths expand during "ACCELERATING" phases (e.g., Sub-theme B in 2024) and narrow during "CONVERGING" phases.
- Sub-sub-themes are depicted as smaller branches or "streams" within the larger ribbons.
- All labels are in sans-serif English text.
- The background is white, maintaining a 16:9 aspect ratio.
- No color names, watercolor effects, or paper counts are included.
Scientific Foundation Models:
## Category Timeline: Scientific Foundation Models

### SUB-THEME A: Scientific Language Pretraining & Knowledge Integration (2018→2024, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"General-to-Scientific Adaptation"** (2018–2019): Transition from general-purpose bidirectional encoders like **BERT** to domain-specific models like **SciBERT**, which utilized 1.14M scientific papers to refine vocabulary for specialized nomenclature.
- **"Non-Parametric Knowledge Retrieval"** (2020–2023): Integration of external knowledge bases through **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)**, combining BART’s parametric memory with Wikipedia-based dense vector indices to reduce hallucinations in knowledge-intensive tasks.
- **"Large-Scale Scientific Synthesis"** (2022–2024): Development of massive models like **Galactica**, designed to store, combine, and reason over 48 million scientific papers, textbooks, and protein sequences as a unified knowledge interface.
- **"Multimodal Data Foundations"** (2024–2025): A shift toward data-centric synthesis as seen in **A Survey of Scientific Large Language Models: From Data Foundations to Discovery**, focusing on how scientific language extends beyond text into chemical strings and biological sequences.
Interaction: FEEDS Sub-theme B by providing the linguistic and structural backbone for domain-specific property prediction; ENABLES Sub-theme C through the development of reasoning capabilities.

### SUB-THEME B: Domain-Specific Discovery & Material Intelligence (2022→2026, ACCELERATING)
Branches into:
- **"Chemical & Molecular Foundation Models"** (2022–2024): Systematic organization of models for property prediction and molecular design, as detailed in **A Perspective on Foundation Models in Chemistry**.
- **"Materials Discovery Frameworks"** (2024–2025): Application of large-scale pretraining to crystal structures and material properties, highlighted in **Foundation Models for Materials Discovery -- Current State and Future Directions**.
- **"Environmental & Earth Science Modeling"** (2025–2026): Expansion of foundation models into forward prediction and data assimilation for climate and ecology, as explored in **Foundation Models for Environmental Science**.
- **"Cross-Domain Biological Synthesis"** (2024–2025): Methodical delineation of "scientific language" within biological and chemical domains to bridge the gap between raw data and actionable wisdom, as seen in **Scientific Large Language Models: A Survey on Biological & Chemical Domains**.
Interaction: RESPONDS to Sub-theme A by applying language architectures to non-textual data; MERGES with Sub-theme C to automate the experimental validation of discovered materials.

### SUB-THEME C: Autonomous Research Agents & Lifecycle Automation (2023→2026, EXPANDING)
Branches into:
- **"Self-Taught Tool Augmentation"** (2023–2024): Models learning to use external APIs (calculators, search engines) via self-supervised methods, pioneered by **Toolformer**.
- **"Scientific Lifecycle Orchestration"** (2024–2025): Systematic automation of the entire research pipeline—from hypothesis generation to experimental execution—as categorized in **AI4Research** and **LLM4SR**.
- **"AI-Assisted Scientific Transformation"** (2025–2026): Curated overview of core techniques for AI-driven discovery, focusing on the shift from human-led to AI-augmented science in **Transforming Science with Large Language Models**.
- **"Case-Driven Research Acceleration"** (2024–2025): Practical deployment of frontier models like **Gemini** to accelerate real-world scientific workflows through case studies in diverse disciplines.
Interaction: SPAWNS Sub-theme D by creating a demand for high-performance, distributed infrastructure to run autonomous research loops.

### SUB-THEME D: Advanced Generative Architectures & Infrastructure (2025→2026, EMERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Verifier-Constrained Flow Expansion"** (2026–2026): Overcoming the density limitations of diffusion models by using external verifiers to explore valid design spaces beyond training data, as seen in **Verifier-Constrained Flow Expansion for Discovery**.
- **"Cross-Facility Federated Learning"** (2026–2026): Scaling foundation models across distributed scientific facilities while maintaining data privacy, as proposed in **Scalable Cross-Facility Federated Learning**.
- **"Scientific Parallelism Techniques"** (2026–2026): Engineering breakthroughs in GPU parallelism specifically optimized for the unique computational demands of scientific LLMs, detailed in **From GPU Engineering to Scientific Discovery**.
- **"Generative AI Era Consolidation"** (2025–2026): Comprehensive reviews of the foundation model era, synthesizing the transition from predictive to generative paradigms in **Generative AI and the Foundation Model Era**.
Interaction: ENABLES the scaling of all previous sub-themes by providing the necessary computational efficiency and architectural robustness.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- **Data-to-Wisdom Pipeline**: The transition from raw scientific data (Sub-theme B) to structured knowledge (Sub-theme A) and finally to autonomous discovery (Sub-theme C) represents the core evolution of the field.
- **Verification & Reliability**: As models move from simple text generation to proposing new materials (Sub-theme B) and autonomous experiments (Sub-theme C), the role of "Verifiers" (Sub-theme D) becomes the critical bottleneck for scientific validity.

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2018: The Transformer Inflection** — **BERT** introduces bidirectional pretraining, establishing the architectural standard for all subsequent scientific language models.
- **2022: The Knowledge Synthesis Shift** — **Galactica** demonstrates that LLMs can act as a primary interface for scientific knowledge, moving beyond simple text processing.
- **2024: The Survey & Consolidation Wave** — A cluster of comprehensive surveys (**Scientific Large Language Models**, **AI4Research**) defines the "Scientific LLM" as a distinct research discipline.
- **2026: The Autonomous Discovery Frontier** — Integration of **Flow Expansion** and **Federated Learning** marks the shift toward models that can discover novel scientific entities beyond the boundaries of their training data.

▶ Scientific Language Pretraining emerges (2018)
◀ General NLP absorbed into Scientific Foundation Models (2024)
Ethics, Policy & Meta-Science:
## Category Timeline: Ethics, Policy & Meta-Science

### SUB-THEME A: AI Impact on Scientific Practice (2022→2026, DIVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"The Productivity-Diversity Paradox"** (2023–2026): Investigates the tension where AI tools expand individual scientist impact but contract the collective scope of inquiry, as evidenced by the analysis of 41.3 million papers showing a narrowing of the "scientific frontier."
- **"Cognitive Debt & Neuro-impact"** (2023–2025): Longitudinal neurophysiological studies using EEG and NLP to measure how LLM-assisted writing affects human brain connectivity and long-term cognitive functions.
- **"AI4Science Conceptual Frameworks"** (2022–2024): Establishing the "Data-to-Wisdom" hierarchy and the Dagstuhl 22382 agenda for bridging data-driven and mechanistic modeling.
- **"Social Science of AI"** (2024–2025): A dual-pathway survey classifying AI as both a tool for social science research and a subject of sociological study.
- **"Economic R&D Task Modeling"** (2023–2025): Theoretical frameworks modeling R&D as a set of heterogeneous tasks to evaluate AI’s impact on innovation economics.
- **"Domain-Specific Adoption Surveys"** (2023–2024): Large-scale usage tracking (e.g., Semantic Scholar authors, Harvard Medical School) to map how researchers integrate LLMs into workflows.
Interaction: **FEEDS** into Peer Review Automation by providing the volume of AI-generated content that necessitates automated oversight.

### SUB-THEME B: Peer Review Automation & Integrity (2023→2025, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"LLM Detection & Forensics"** (2023–2025): Systematic investigation of AI-generated text in ICLR reviews and the development of 99%+ accuracy domain-specific detectors for admissions materials.
- **"NLP Problem Space Mapping"** (2024–2025): The Dagstuhl 24052 white paper effort to categorize every stage of the peer review process where NLP can contribute or intervene.
- **"Ethical Sentiment & Policy"** (2023–2024): Global surveys (e.g., Nature’s 5,000-researcher study) revealing deep divisions in the community regarding the legitimacy of AI in manuscript evaluation.
- **"Data Integrity Frameworks"** (2024–2026): Development of multi-faceted frameworks to combat the "integrity crisis" in fields like materials science caused by AI-accelerated data fabrication.
- **"Automated Admissions Screening"** (2023–2025): Specialized research into detecting AI-modified vs. AI-generated personal statements and recommendation letters.
Interaction: **RESPONDS** to the "Productivity Paradox" by attempting to filter the resulting surge in scientific output.

### SUB-THEME C: AI Agent Reliability & Human-AI Collaboration (2024→2026, EMERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Reliability Science for Agents"** (2024–2026): The transition from simple LLM tools to a formal "Science of AI Agent Reliability" focusing on predictable behavior in autonomous research agents.
- **"Human-AI Thought Partnership"** (2024–2026): Designing AI not as a tool for thought but as a "thought partner" capable of collaborative reasoning and learning.
- **"Labor Market & Agent Audits"** (2024–2025): Large-scale audits of 844 occupations using the O*NET database to distinguish between task automation and human augmentation.
- **"AI-Augmented Inventorship Law"** (2025–2026): Methodologies for validating human inventorship in AI-driven molecular discovery through "Audit Gates" for patent documentation.
- **"Security & Code Vulnerability"** (2023–2025): Empirical studies on OpenAI Codex and GitHub Copilot showing that AI assistants can lead developers to produce more insecure code.
Interaction: **SPAWNS** from the maturation of LLMs into agentic workflows, requiring new legal and safety standards.

### SUB-THEME D: Open Access & Publishing Reform (2015→2024, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"OA Economic Feasibility"** (2015–2024): Long-term financial analysis proving that global subscription budgets are sufficient to cover a total transition to Open Access.
- **"Disciplinary Barriers Meta-synthesis"** (2023–2024): Analysis of 11 bibliometric studies identifying why OA adoption varies across academic disciplines.
- **"Search Engine Hegemony"** (2018–2024): Comparative studies of 12 academic databases (ASEBDs) showing Google Scholar’s increasing dominance over traditional citation indexes.
- **"Institutional Transition Models"** (2021–2024): Case studies of Norwegian SSH journals and Oxford University Press (OUP) reflecting the shift from subscription to OA.
Interaction: **ENABLES** the AI Impact theme by providing the massive, open-access datasets required for training AI4Science models.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- **Data Integrity ↔ AI Agents**: The rise of autonomous agents (Sub-theme C) creates new challenges for data integrity (Sub-theme B), as agents may inadvertently hallucinate or bypass human verification steps.
- **Open Access ↔ AI Impact**: The success of OA publishing (Sub-theme D) provides the raw material for the AI-driven expansion of scientific impact (Sub-theme A), but also facilitates the "contraction" of research diversity through algorithmic bias.

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2015: The OA Financial Proof** — Demonstration that the subscription model's total cost could fully fund a global Open Access ecosystem, triggering a decade of policy shifts.
- **2023: The Generative Shock** — Nature declares ChatGPT a "force for good and evil," marking the point where LLMs moved from niche tools to central scientific disruptors.
- **2025: AAAI Presidential Panel Report** — 24 leading experts and 475 community members formalize the future roadmap for AI research ethics and policy.

▶ **AI Agent Reliability** emerges as a distinct scientific discipline (**2024**)
◀ **Subscription Publishing** absorbed into **Open Access Models** (**2024**)

### ABSOLUTE VISUAL RULES (FOR IMPLEMENTATION)
- Every ribbon MUST have its sub-theme name labeled.
- English only, horizontal left→right, years on top.
- River delta style, organic flowing ribbons.
- White background, sans-serif.
- Show band WIDTH changes to reflect paper volume/intensity.
Other:
## Category Timeline: Other

### SUB-THEME A: Mathematical Foundations & Structural Efficiency (2018→2024, CONVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Continuous-Depth Modeling"** (2018–2021): Introduction of **Neural Ordinary Differential Equations** as a new family of deep learning models, shifting from discrete layers to continuous-time dynamics.
- **"Generalized Multi-view Clustering"** (2022–2023): Development of the **Align then Fusion** framework to solve large-scale anchor-based clustering through flexible structural alignment.
- **"Reliability & Uncertainty Quantification"** (2023–2024): Systematic categorization of **Uncertainty Quantification Methods** for DNNs, distinguishing between data and model uncertainty to improve safety.
- **"Efficient State-Space Inference"** (2024–2024): Transitioning to **State-Free Inference of State-Space Models** via the **Transfer Function Approach** to bypass memory bottlenecks in large state sizes.
Interaction: FEEDs into Sub-theme C by providing the mathematical reliability and computational efficiency required for autonomous agent logic.

### SUB-THEME B: Human-Centric Systems & Applied AI Workflows (2021→2025, DIVERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Social-Mobility Architecture"** (2021–2022): Designing **Architecture for Human-Driven Systems** that integrates social behavior into continuous engineering cycles.
- **"Semi-Supervised Vision"** (2023–2024): Advancing **2D Human Pose Estimation** using **Position Inconsistency** to leverage unlabeled data in human-centric spatial tasks.
- **"AI-Augmented Research"** (2023–2025): Practical integration of AI tools for hypothesis generation and literature review as outlined in the **AI for Research: Ultimate Guide**.
- **"Agentic Adoption Patterns"** (2024–2025): Empirical analysis of user behavior through the **Adoption and Usage of AI Agents** study, focusing on the **Perplexity Comet** browser.
Interaction: RESPONDs to the foundational shifts in Sub-theme A by applying them to real-world human environments and academic productivity.

### SUB-THEME C: Autonomous Agents & Cognitive Logic (2024→2025, EMERGING)
Branches into:
- **"Multi-Agent Supply Chains"** (2024–2025): Implementation of the **A2SC methodology** for **autonomous supply chains**, moving from manual logistics to multi-agent system (MAS) orchestration.
- **"Industrial MAS Case Studies"** (2024–2025): Validation of autonomous frameworks in specific sectors, such as the **autonomous meat supply chain** case study.
- **"Computational Reasoning"** (2025–2025): Formalization of **Reason** as a capacity for logic-based conclusion drawing from existing data within AI architectures.
- **"Logic-Driven Decision Making"** (2025–2025): Integration of conscious logic application into autonomous agents to ensure valid conclusions in complex environments.
Interaction: MERGEs the structural efficiency of SSMs from Sub-theme A with the user-centric insights of Sub-theme B to ENABLE fully autonomous industrial operations.

### CROSS-CUTTING CONNECTIONS
- Mathematical efficiency in **State-Space Models** directly enables the real-time processing required for **Autonomous Supply Chains**.
- **Uncertainty Quantification** frameworks provide the safety layer necessary for **Human-Driven Systems** and high-stakes **AI for Research**.
- The transition from **Neural ODEs** to **Reason**-based logic represents a shift from purely statistical patterns to structured, continuous, and logical inference.

### KEY MILESTONES
- **2018: Neural ODE Breakthrough** — The introduction of continuous-depth models redefines the fundamental structure of neural networks.
- **2024: Autonomous Supply Chain Realization** — The shift from theoretical multi-agent systems to the practical **A2SC** implementation in industrial logistics.
▶ Computational Logic emerges (2025)
◀ Manual System Engineering absorbed into Autonomous MAS (2024)
